title: Fix Webhook 0001 Sending Wrong User Record on Signup
description:
Fix a bug in the 0001 webhook (First Time Login) where the event payload was sending incorrect user data. During an end-to-end test, a new user signed up via the Gravity Form (entry ID 417) and successfully logged in, but the webhook payload contained the wrong email address and missing GFEntryId.

### Observed Behavior
- New user created: rgoodman@reliantfunding.com (user ID 71, team ID 55)
- Webhook sent: ryan@goodmangroupllc.com with empty GFEntryId and teamAdminUserId
- The webhook appeared to be picking up a different user record from the database

### Expected Behavior
- Webhook should send the newly created user's details with the correct GFEntryId (417) from their Gravity Form submission
acceptance_criteria: Webhook 0001 sends the correct user email matching the newly authenticated user
GFEntryId is populated with the Gravity Form entry ID for web-to-lead signups
TeamAdminUserID is correctly null for non-invited users
User lookup in webhook correctly identifies the newly created user
middleware_changes: Fixed the user lookup logic in the 0001 webhook handler to correctly identify the newly created/authenticated user instead of picking up a stale or incorrect record.
implementation_notes: The bug caused the webhook to reference the wrong user record. The Salesforce username stored was `rgoodman@reliantfunding.com` (without the `.prod` suffix used as the email key). Fix ensured the webhook pulls data from the currently authenticated user session rather than a database lookup that could return the wrong record.
comment_summary: Product owner provided detailed end-to-end test results showing the mismatch. Developer fixed and product owner validated the correct payload was being sent.
